listen_host.description: Interface the WebSocket server binds to. Defaults to all interfaces for LAN access from the puck.

listen_port.description: TCP port for the device WebSocket server.

device_shared_secret.description: Shared secret every device sends in its hello frame. Auto-generated on first setup; copy into the firmware NVS image. v1 uses one secret across all devices; per-device keys are v2.

display_style.description: How the round LCD renders states. 'minimal' shows centered icons + clock; 'orb' shows an Echo-style pulsing ring.

wake_word_model.description: WakeNet model ID the firmware should load. v1 ships the stock 'hi_esp' model; a custom 'hey_gurney' model can be swapped in later without protocol changes.

owner_chat_id.description: Telegram chat ID that device turns post into, so the puck shares conversation history (and memory) with the user's normal chat.

vad_silence_ms.description: Server-side voice activity detector: this many milliseconds of low-RMS audio closes a turn.

max_utterance_sec.description: Hard cap on a single spoken turn. Long-tail rambles get transcribed up to this length and then the turn closes.

tts_voice_id.description: Optional Piper voice ID override for device replies. Falls back to gurney-voice's voice_id when unset.

whisper_bin.description: Path or name of the whisper.cpp CLI. Defaults to the same binary gurney-voice installs.

whisper_model_path.description: Filesystem path to a ggml whisper model (.bin). Point at the model gurney-voice already downloaded — typically ~/.gurney/extension_state/gurney-voice/whisper-models/ggml-base.en.bin.

piper_bin.description: Path or name of the piper executable. Defaults to the same binary gurney-voice installs.

ffmpeg_bin.description: Path or name of ffmpeg, used by Piper TTS to produce OGG/Opus for device playback.

voice_model_path.description: Filesystem path to the Piper voice model (.onnx). Point at the same file gurney-voice uses.

llm_profile.description: Which LLM profile to use for device replies. 'chat' is the small, fast model — appropriate for voice latency. v0.1 talks to host.llm directly; tool-using orchestrator turns are deferred to a later release.

llm_max_tokens.description: Cap the model reply at this many tokens. Voice replies should be short — long monologues are punishing to listen to.

system_prompt.default: You are Gurney, a friendly voice assistant speaking through a small home device. Reply in one or two short spoken sentences. No code blocks, no markdown.
system_prompt.description: System prompt for the LLM. Used only by the direct-LLM dispatch fallback (no owner_chat_id). The orchestrator path uses Gurney's main system prompt + the Telegram-tuned prompt fragments.

speech_max_chars.description: Hard cap on the spoken reply length. Replies longer than this get cut on the nearest sentence boundary. 600 chars ≈ 30 seconds spoken — voice latency punishes anything longer.

volume_default.description: Default playback volume (0.0-1.0). Applied the first time a device connects; subsequent connects replay whatever volume the device last reported.
